briefdescription.para: The type of the model is unknown.
The model defines a system of algebraic equations that can be solved directly.
The model defines a system of differential algebraic equations that require both an external ODE solver (e.g., CVODE) and an external NLA solver (e.g., KINSOL) to solve.
The model is invalid.
The model defines a system of (potentially non-linear) algebraic equations that require an external NLA solver (e.g., KINSOL) to solve.
The model defines a system of ordinary differential equations that require an external ODE solver (e.g., CVODE) to solve.
The model is determined to be overconstrained as there are more equations than unknown variables.
The model is determined to be underconstrained as there are insufficient equations to constrain all variables.
The model is determined to be unsuitably constrained, i.e.

detaileddescription.para: both overconstrained and underconstrained.

briefdescription.para: The type of a model.

detaileddescription.para: The type of a model, i.e. whether it is unknown, invalid, underconstrained, overconstrained, or unsuitably constrained, or whether it defines a system of algebraic equations, a system of non-linear algebraic equations, a system of ordinary differential equations, or a system of differential algebraic equations.

briefdescription.para: Private member to implementation pointer,.
Destructor,.
Copy constructor,.
Move constructor,.
Assignment operator,.
Constructor,.
Test to determine if AnalyserModel is a valid model.

detaileddescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel is a valid model, return true if it is a valid model (i.e. either an algebraic or an ODE model) and false otherwise.

simplesect.para: true if AnalyserModel is a valid model, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Get the Type of the AnalyserModel.

detaileddescription.para: Return the Type of the AnalyserModel.

simplesect.para: The Type.

briefdescription.para: Test to determine if this AnalyserModel has external variables.

detaileddescription.para: Test to determine if this AnalyserModel has external variables, return true if it does and false otherwise.

simplesect.para: true if AnalyserModel has external variables, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Get the Variable of integration.

detaileddescription.para: Return the Variable of integration of the AnalyserModel, in the case of an ODE model, nullptr otherwise.

simplesect.para: The Variable of integration, if an ODE model, nullptr otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Get the number of states.

detaileddescription.para: Return the number of states in the AnalyserModel.

simplesect.para: The number of states.

briefdescription.para: Get the states.

detaileddescription.para: Return the states in the AnalyserModel.

simplesect.para: The states as a std::vector.

briefdescription.para: Get the state at index.

detaileddescription.para: Return the state at the index index in this AnalyserModel.

parameterdescription.para: The index of the state to return.

simplesect.para: The state at the given index on success, nullptr on failure.

briefdescription.para: Get the number of constants.

detaileddescription.para: Return the number of constants in the AnalyserModel.

simplesect.para: The number of constants.

briefdescription.para: Get the constants.

detaileddescription.para: Return the constants in the AnalyserModel.

simplesect.para: The constants as a std::vector.

briefdescription.para: Get the constant at index.

detaileddescription.para: Return the constant at the index index in this AnalyserModel.

parameterdescription.para: The index of the constant to return.

simplesect.para: The constant at the given index on success, nullptr on failure.

briefdescription.para: Get the number of computed constants.

detaileddescription.para: Return the number of computed constants in the AnalyserModel.

simplesect.para: The number of computed constants.

briefdescription.para: Get the computed constants.

detaileddescription.para: Return the computed constants in the AnalyserModel.

simplesect.para: The computed constants as a std::vector.

briefdescription.para: Get the computed constant at index.

detaileddescription.para: Return the computed constant at the index index in this AnalyserModel.

parameterdescription.para: The index of the computed constant to return.

simplesect.para: The computed constant at the given index on success, nullptr on failure.

briefdescription.para: Get the number of algebraic variables.

detaileddescription.para: Return the number of algebraic variables in the AnalyserModel.

simplesect.para: The number of algebraic variables.

briefdescription.para: Get the algebraic variables.

detaileddescription.para: Return the algebraic variables in the AnalyserModel.

simplesect.para: The algebraic variables as a std::vector.

briefdescription.para: Get the algebraic variable at index.

detaileddescription.para: Return the algebraic variable at the index index in this AnalyserModel.

parameterdescription.para: The index of the algebraic variable to return.

simplesect.para: The algebraic variable at the given index on success, nullptr on failure.

briefdescription.para: Get the number of external variables.

detaileddescription.para: Return the number of external variables in the AnalyserModel.

simplesect.para: The number of external variables.

briefdescription.para: Get the external variables.

detaileddescription.para: Return the external variables in the AnalyserModel.

simplesect.para: The external variables as a std::vector.

briefdescription.para: Get the external variable at index.

detaileddescription.para: Return the external variable at the index index in this AnalyserModel.

parameterdescription.para: The index of the external variable to return.

simplesect.para: The external variable at the given index on success, nullptr on failure.

briefdescription.para: Get the analyser variable for the given variable.

detaileddescription.para: Return the analyser variable for the given variable in this AnalyserModel.

parameterdescription.para: The variable for which to return the analyser variable.

simplesect.para: The analyser variable for the given variable on success, nullptr on failure.

briefdescription.para: Get the number of analyser equations.

detaileddescription.para: Return the number of analyser equations in the AnalyserModel.

simplesect.para: The number of analyser equations.

briefdescription.para: Get the analyser equations.

detaileddescription.para: Return the analyser equations in the AnalyserModel.

simplesect.para: The analyser equations as a std::vector.

briefdescription.para: Get the analyser equation at index.

detaileddescription.para: Return the analyser equation at the index index in this AnalyserModel.

parameterdescription.para: The index of the analyser equation to return.

simplesect.para: The analyser equation at the given index on success, nullptr on failure.

briefdescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs an "equal to" function.

detaileddescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs an "equal to" function, return true if it does and false otherwise.

simplesect.para: true if AnalyserModel needs an "equal to" function, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs a "not equal to" function.

detaileddescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs a "not equal to" function, return true if it does and false otherwise.

simplesect.para: true if AnalyserModel needs a "not equal to" function, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs a "less than" function.

detaileddescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs a "less than" function, return true if it does and false otherwise.

simplesect.para: true if AnalyserModel needs a "less than" function, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs a "less than or equal to" function.

detaileddescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs a "less than or equal to" function, return true if it does and false otherwise.

simplesect.para: true if AnalyserModel needs a "less than or equal to" function, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs a "greater than" function.

detaileddescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs a "greater than" function, return true if it does and false otherwise.

simplesect.para: true if AnalyserModel needs a "greater than" function, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs a "greater than or equal to" function.

detaileddescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs a "greater than or equal to" function, return true if it does and false otherwise.

simplesect.para: true if AnalyserModel needs a "greater than or equal to" function, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs an "and" function.

detaileddescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs an "and" function, return true if it does and false otherwise.

simplesect.para: true if AnalyserModel needs an "and" function, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs an "or" function.

detaileddescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs an "or" function, return true if it does and false otherwise.

simplesect.para: true if AnalyserModel needs an "or" function, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs a "exclusive or" function.

detaileddescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs a "exclusive or" function, return true if it does and false otherwise.

simplesect.para: true if AnalyserModel needs a "exclusive or" function, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs a "not" function.

detaileddescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs a "not" function, return true if it does and false otherwise.

simplesect.para: true if AnalyserModel needs a "not" function, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs a "minimum" function.

detaileddescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs a "minimum" function, return true if it does and false otherwise.

simplesect.para: true if AnalyserModel needs a "minimum" function, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs a "maximum" function.

detaileddescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs a "maximum" function, return true if it does and false otherwise.

simplesect.para: true if AnalyserModel needs a "maximum" function, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs a "secant" function.

detaileddescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs a "secant" function, return true if it does and false otherwise.

simplesect.para: true if AnalyserModel needs a "secant" function, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs a "cosecant" function.

detaileddescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs a "cosecant" function, return true if it does and false otherwise.

simplesect.para: true if AnalyserModel needs a "cosecant" function, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs a "cotangent" function.

detaileddescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs a "cotangent" function, return true if it does and false otherwise.

simplesect.para: true if AnalyserModel needs a "cotangent" function, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs a "hyperbolic secant" function.

detaileddescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs a "hyperbolic secant" function, return true if it does and false otherwise.

simplesect.para: true if AnalyserModel needs a "hyperbolic secant" function, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs a "hyperbolic cosecant" function.

detaileddescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs a "hyperbolic cosecant" function, return true if it does and false otherwise.

simplesect.para: true if AnalyserModel needs a "hyperbolic cosecant" function, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs a "hyperbolic cotangent" function.

briefdescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs an "arc secant" function.

detaileddescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs an "arc secant" function, return true if it does and false otherwise.

simplesect.para: true if AnalyserModel needs an "arc secant" function, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs an "arc cosecant" function.

briefdescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs an "arc cotangent" function.

detaileddescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs an "arc cotangent" function, return true if it does and false otherwise.

simplesect.para: true if AnalyserModel needs an "arc cotangent" function, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs an "arc hyperbolic secant" function.

detaileddescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs an "arc hyperbolic secant" function, return true if it does and false otherwise.

simplesect.para: true if AnalyserModel needs an "arc hyperbolic secant" function, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs an "arc hyperbolic cosecant" function.

detaileddescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs an "arc hyperbolic cosecant" function, return true if it does and false otherwise.

simplesect.para: true if AnalyserModel needs an "arc hyperbolic cosecant" function, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs an "arc hyperbolic cotangent" function.

detaileddescription.para: Test to determine if AnalyserModel needs an "arc hyperbolic cotangent" function, return true if it does and false otherwise.

simplesect.para: true if AnalyserModel needs an "arc hyperbolic cotangent" function, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Test to determine if variable1 and variable2 are equivalent.

detaileddescription.para: Test to see if variable1 is the same as or equivalent to variable2. Returns true if variable1 is equivalent to variable2 and false otherwise.

To test for equivalence is time consuming, so caching is used to speed things up. During the analysis of a model, various tests are performed and their result cached. So, if you test two variables that were tested during the analysis then the cached result will be returned otherwise the two variables will be properly tested and their result cached. This works because an AnalyserModel always refers to a static version of a Model. However, this might break if a Model is modified after it has been analysed.

parameterdescription.para: The Variable to test if it is equivalent to variable2.
The Variable that is potentially equivalent to variable1.

simplesect.para: true if variable1 is equivalent to variable2 and false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Get the string version of a Type.

detaileddescription.para: Return the string version of a Type.

parameterdescription.para: The type for which we want the string version.

simplesect.para: The string version of the Type.

briefdescription.para: The AnalyserModel class.

detaileddescription.para: The AnalyserModel class is for representing a model in the context of a CellML Analyser.
